Package global.thalion.ttio

Class StreamWriter

java.lang.Object
global.thalion.ttio.StreamWriter
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable
public final class StreamWriter extends Object implements AutoCloseable
Incrementally append mass spectra to an .tio file.

Spectra accumulate in memory until flush() is called. On each flush the file is rewritten so that the run group reflects every spectrum buffered so far — the file remains a valid .tio after each flush.

For v0.6 the writer's flush is whole-file regenerative: simple, correct, and bounded for the streaming-demo case (≤ a few thousand spectra). A future milestone may switch to extendable HDF5 datasets.

API status: Stable.

Cross-language equivalents: Objective-C TTIOStreamWriter, Python ttio.stream_writer.StreamWriter.

    • StreamWriter

  • Method Details

    • appendSpectrum

      public void appendSpectrum(MassSpectrum spectrum)

    • spectrumCount

      public int spectrumCount()

    • flush

      public void flush()
      Rewrite the target file with all buffered spectra so far.

      Flattens the buffered spectra into concatenated m/z and intensity arrays, builds a SpectrumIndex and an AcquisitionRun, then delegates to SpectralDataset.create(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.util.List<global.thalion.ttio.AcquisitionRun>, java.util.List<global.thalion.ttio.Identification>, java.util.List<global.thalion.ttio.Quantification>, java.util.List<global.thalion.ttio.ProvenanceRecord>) so the file is a valid .tio after each call.

    • flushAndClose

      public void flushAndClose()

    • close

      public void close()
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
